message:
feat(layout,core): fix rank coordinate assignment, add extended shapes, improve parser routing

Layout (fm-layout/lib.rs):
  Rewrite coordinate_assignment() to compute rank positions from actual
  node sizes rather than using a fixed rank*spacing formula. Previously,
  all ranks were positioned as if every node had the same size, causing
  wide nodes in LR layouts to overlap their neighbors. The new approach:
  1. Collects the maximum primary-axis extent per rank
  2. Builds cumulative offsets so each rank starts after the previous
     rank's tallest/widest node plus the configured rank spacing
  3. Uses rank_to_index mapping to handle sparse rank numbering
  This fixes the bug where LR layouts with mixed-width nodes (e.g. a
  short label next to a long label in the same column) produced
  overlapping or misaligned columns.

  Also add three new tests:
  - vertical_routing_adds_turn_for_offset_nodes: validates L-shaped
    edge routing between non-colinear nodes in TB direction
  - horizontal_routing_adds_turn_for_offset_nodes: same for LR
  - lr_same_rank_nodes_with_different_widths_share_column_position:
    regression test for the coordinate assignment fix

Core (fm-core/lib.rs):
  Add font_metrics module integration and 7 new NodeShape variants
  (InvTrapezoid, Triangle, Pentagon, Star, Cloud, Tag, CrossedCircle)
  needed by the extended Mermaid v11 shape syntax. These map to the
  shapes that mermaid.js supports but our renderer previously could not
  draw, causing fallback-to-rect for legitimate diagram input.

Parser (fm-parser):
  - Route DOT-format inputs through detect_type() so they return
    DiagramType::Flowchart instead of falling through to the mermaid
    detector (which would misclassify them or return Unknown)
  - Add test: detects_dot_inputs_as_flowchart
  - Formatting cleanup (rustfmt import ordering, assert macro style)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
